source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A waveguide is formed by two infinite parallel perfectly conducting planes separated by a distance $a$. The gap between the planes is filled with a gas whose index of refraction is $n$ (this is taken to be frequency independent). Any electromagnetic disturbance (independent of $y$) must be expressible as a superposition of the waveguide modes considered in part (a). What is the mode corresponding to the principal frequency of the Čerenkov spectrum considered in part (b)?
extraction_notes: question references part (a) and part (b), so some context is missing from this single record

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A waveguide is formed by two infinite parallel perfectly conducting planes separated by a distance $a$. The gap between the planes is filled with a gas whose index of refraction is $n$ (this is taken to be frequency independent). Any electromagnetic disturbance (independent of $y$) must be expressible as a superposition of the waveguide modes considered in part (a). candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

metadata.reasoning_chain: Let $S$ be the unit vector normal to the upper plane of the wedge which forms the surface of the Cerenkov radiation, with $\mathbf{k}$ in that direction and $|\mathbf{k}|=\frac{n\omega}{c}$. Then we have $k_{x}=\frac{\omega}{c}n\sin\varphi=\frac{\omega}{c}n\left(\frac{c}{nv}\right)=\frac{\omega}{v}$. Since only those frequencies that satisfy $\omega=\frac{c}{n}\sqrt{\left(\frac{\omega}{v}\right)^2+\left(\frac{m\pi}{a}\right)^2}$ can propagate in the waveguide, solving this relation for $\omega$ leads to $\omega=\frac{m\pi}{a}\left(\frac{n^2}{c^2}-\frac{1}{v^2}\right)^{-1/2}$ for $m=1,2,\ldots$.
